description: Street-level computer vision nodes: fetch Google Street View imagery, run HuggingFace segmentation/detection models on the panoramas, and inspect the results in a rich gallery. The catalog install will pip-install torch/transformers/ultralytics on first install. Users paste their Google Maps API key directly into the Street View Fetcher node.
name: Street Vision

templates.description: Geocode a place name, sample Google Street View imagery inside its bounding box, and emit a GEODATAFRAME of image points (each feature carrying image_url + lat/lon). Reusable for anyone needing Street View panoramas without inference.
templates.label: Street View Fetcher

templates.description: Run HuggingFace segmentation or detection models on a collection of image points. Input: a GEODATAFRAME whose features carry image_url (any source works — Street View Fetcher, Data Loading, or a custom Python node). Output: per-image inference results as JSON.
templates.label: HF CV Inference

templates.description: Inspect CV inference results: image gallery, segmentation overlays, side-by-side compare, per-image class breakdowns, and aggregate stats. Re-emits the same payload downstream as a GEODATAFRAME so subsequent nodes (Spatial Join, Vega-Lite, AUTK Map, …) can consume it.
templates.label: CV Gallery
